author: kevaundray
content: Reading on mobile, but writeup looks great!

author: qu0b
content: do we need to include the BAL here?

author: thegaram33
content:
Somewhat of an open question, I think we could consider dropping BAL.

This is the rationale from EIP-8142:

> Why BALs? While BALs are technically the output of executing transactions and could be recomputed, once zkEVM proofs become mandatory for consensus, validators no longer execute payloads. A malicious builder could publish a valid proof and withhold both the execution payload data and the BALs. This would prevent other builders from constructing subsequent blocks and prevent RPC providers from serving the full state. Including BALs in payload-blobs ensures they remain available.

author: kevaundray
content: I was thinking that in most cases, a builder would want to apply the BAL instead of re-executing the block, which is why it’s useful to have it always be available — I don’t have a strong opinion on taking it out given how hard it is to compute payload blob count with it

author: kevaundray
content: A different consideration is changing BALs so that it’s easier to know its size while building?

author: thegaram33
content:
Two nice-to-haves:
1. Know the BAL size while building => We can predict `payload_blob_count` and make sure we don't go over the blob count limit.
2. Serialize BAL incrementally => We can pipeline block building and KZG proof computation.

Afaik any transaction can update the BAL, so structurally I'm not sure these are achievable.

But this could just be an implementation painpoint (not a blocker for BiB) because builders are already sophisticated entities.

author: omerfirmak
content:
> Know the BAL size while building

Isn't it possible to incrementally calculate at least an upper bound? It shouldn't be hard to figure out how many entries a tx will add to BAL and how many additional worst-case-bytes that will correspond to.

author: thegaram33
content: Right, that's what I did in the prototype. It's doable, though the gap between the actual size and the upper bound might be large (mainly when transactions add overlapping BAL entries).
